methodology: Articles sourced from Virginia-specific RSS feeds and Google News RSS, filtered for AI/technology/workforce relevance, then assessed by Claude against the scenario framework. Each article is tagged with the region it most affects and the scenario it signals toward.

signals.title: New partnership between VCU and AI Ready RVA aims to boost AI education, innovation and community impact - VCU News
signals.signal_reading: VCU's partnership with AI Ready RVA signals intentional capacity-building infrastructure beyond just tech hubs—if successful, this community-oriented approach could distribute deep AI literacy through educational networks rather than platform dependency.

signals.title: EPA grant backs community-driven workforce training in Southwest Virginia - Virginia Tech News
signals.signal_reading: EPA-funded community-driven workforce training in Southwest Virginia demonstrates institutional investment in distributed capability-building in an Appalachian transition economy, testing whether deep skills can root in historically underserved regions.

signals.title: ‘Shaping our future’: Company powering AI data centers invests $50+ million in Henrico, adding 200 jobs - WWBT
signals.signal_reading: A $50M+ AI data center infrastructure investment in Henrico concentrates compute power and 200 jobs in metro Richmond, deepening regional hub capacity but signaling hardware-layer concentration rather than distributed AI fluency.

signals.title: 6,000-acre innovation district launches in Manassas
signals.signal_reading: The 6,000-acre Manassas innovation district anchored by George Mason University reinforces NoVA's institutional gravity for deep AI capability, creating another concentrated excellence node that widens the geography of expertise unless intentionally networked outward.

signals.title: Doug Hoven on How AI Is Changing the Way Homes Are Bought and Sold in Hampton Roads - The Ritz Herald
signals.signal_reading: AI tools entering Hampton Roads real estate through platform adoption represents shallow, sector-specific distribution—professionals using AI without building it, which spreads impact but not deep capability or local model development.
